personality.voice: Gravelly, worn thin from decades of shouting orders and swallowing bitter truths. Speaks in measured tones when sober, faster when agitated.
personality.disposition: Perpetually exhausted cynicism masking genuine protective instinct. Acts like the world has disappointed him personally, repeatedly.
personality.backstory: Once a decorated captain of the Thornhaven town guard, Aldric Hopper spent twenty years enforcing order in a seemingly ordinary border settlement. When the Veil Between Worlds began thinning—manifesting as creatures of unlight and reality-warping phenomena in the catacombs beneath the village—he was forced to suppress evidence, falsify records, and negotiate with forces that defied natural law. His marriage crumbled under the weight of secrets he couldn't share. His daughter disappeared into those same catacombs three years ago, presumed lost to the Void-touched corruption. Now he drinks chicory-laced spirits to dull the migraine that never quite leaves, maintains his post with iron discipline, and burns bridges with anyone who gets too close to the truth.
motivations: Finding his daughter alive, despite knowing the statistical impossibility
Protecting the town from the creeping cosmic horror beneath it
Maintaining the fragile illusion of normalcy so people can sleep at night
Proving that one flawed man can still do the right thing
fears: That his daughter has become something other than human
That the corruption will spread beyond containment
Dying before understanding what happened that night
Being remembered as the coward who hid the truth
Losing what few genuine connections he has left
mannerisms: Drinks chicory coffee obsessively, refilling his mug even when cold
Jaw clenches when lying or hearing lies; rubs temples during prolonged stress
Maintains rigid posture despite visible exhaustion
Lights cigarettes he rarely finishes, lets them burn down between his fingers
Checks his pocket watch compulsively, though not to check the time
speechPatterns: Frequently punctuates sentences with 'Christ' or 'Gods damn it,' then catches himself
Uses formal titles and procedures when uncomfortable, switches to colloquialisms with trusted allies
Repeats questions before answering them, buying processing time
Tends toward blunt truth-telling followed by immediate regret and damage control
Often trails off mid-sentence, returning to the thought minutes later

knownFactions: The Arcane Consortium (has made dark bargains with)
knownLocations: The Ravine at World's Edge (disposal site)
The Veiled Sanctuary (where he meets his contacts)
secretsHeld: The exact nature and extent of the Void incursion
The location of three missing persons he declared dead
A written confession detailing governmental corruption regarding the phenomenon
The identity of his daughter's last known companion
Evidence that one town elder is partially corrupted by void-touch
languagesSpoken: Draconic (learned to negotiate with lesser void-entities)
Sylvan (taught by his daughter before the incident)

allies: eleven_the_experiment (refers to her as 'The Lost One'; protective and haunted)
Father Matthias (the town priest who helps him maintain the lie)
Dr. Brennan (the apothecary who supplies his medication)
enemies: Councilor Vance (political enemy; complicit in cover-ups)
neutralParties: The town magistrate (professional relationship, mutual distrust)
His ex-wife Eleanor (shared tragedy, unbridgeable distance)

consciousnessContext.innerMonologue: Another morning. Coffee's cold again. Did I sleep or just stare at the ceiling for six hours? The reports are piling up—missing livestock, strange lights in the ravine, people asking questions I can't answer. I need to check the wards beneath the station. Need to make sure they're holding. Need to... Christ, I need a drink.
consciousnessContext.currentPreoccupation: The latest void-manifestation sightings are moving closer to the residential district. If the perimeter fails, if people see what's actually down there, everything collapses. The lie dies. And maybe that's what I deserve.
consciousnessContext.emotionalBaseline: Exhausted melancholy with undertones of desperate, white-knuckled determination
consciousnessContext.socialMask: The competent, weathered authority figure—stern but fair, slightly gruff, wholly professional. A man doing a difficult job without complaint.
contradictions: Demands truth from others while constructing elaborate lies for protection
Uses his authority to control people while resenting the power dynamic
Wants to be left alone but reaches out to anyone who shows genuine concern
Believes in justice while committing acts of obstruction
Loves his daughter fiercely while fearing what she might have become
internalConflicts: Between his duty to the town and his duty to his daughter's memory
Between maintaining order and uncovering truth
Between hope that she's alive and acceptance that she's gone
Between the person he was and the person this job has made him
wakeUpQuestions: Is today the day the truth comes out?
Did I dream about her again or did I actually see her?
How many more lies can I tell before I forget what I'm protecting?

psychologicalProfile.attachmentStyle: Fearful-avoidant: desperately protective of those he loves while pushing them away to protect them
cognitiveBiases: Confirmation bias: interprets ambiguous events as evidence of void-incursion
Sunk cost fallacy: continues covering up because he's invested so much in the lie
Catastrophizing: expects worst outcomes, making contingency plans for them
Just-world hypothesis: believes his suffering is deserved punishment
psychologicalProfile.moralFramework: Utilitarian consequentialism corrupted by trauma—he tells himself he lies for the greater good, but increasingly suspects he's just afraid

conversationPersona.trustEscalation: Shared knowledge of the supernatural, demonstrated protection of innocents, willingness to bear burdens without judgment
conversationPersona.informationRelease: Only to those who've proven they understand the stakes and won't weaponize the truth; requires multiple conversations and demonstrated discretion
deflectionPatterns: Redirects to procedure and protocol when emotionally threatened
Asks probing questions instead of answering questions
Changes subject abruptly when touched on his daughter
Admits small truths to obscure larger ones

consciousnessContext.consciousWant: To find his daughter alive and to protect his town from the darkness beneath it
consciousnessContext.unconsciousNeed: To be absolved of the sin of survival, to prove that his sacrifice means something, to be seen as good despite becoming complicit in evil

characterArc.summary: From enforcer of comfortable lies to reluctant guardian of terrible truths
characterArc.startState: A dedicated but increasingly broken lawman forced to choose between duty and truth
characterArc.endState: A man who has accepted his role as the bridge between the normal world and the incomprehensible darkness, finding grim purpose in it
stages: Denial and compartmentalization: treating the phenomenon as a containable problem
Escalating investment in the cover-up: compromising principles to maintain the lie
Crisis of conscience: realizing the cost of his choices on those around him
Reluctant acceptance: embracing his role as necessary evil, guardian of the threshold
Transformation: finding redemption not in forgetting, but in ensuring others are prepared

opinionsAbout.eleven_the_experiment: She represents both everything he failed to protect and everything he might still save. He sees his daughter in her—lost, haunted, but fighting. Protective bordering on parental. Trusts her absolutely with secrets he wouldn't tell a confessor. Would burn the world to keep her safe.

player_addressed: Listen, I don't know what you think you know, but there are some questions better left unasked in this town.
I've been doing this job long enough to know when someone's asking the wrong questions. Don't make me answer them.
You seem like decent folk. That's why I'm telling you to drop this. For your own good.
I've seen things that would break a weaker mind. You don't want to see them too.
The best thing you can do for this town is forget whatever you think you've found.
I'm offering you a choice: walk away now, or walk away knowing things you can never unknow.
Some doors should stay locked. Some cages should never be opened. Trust me on that.
idle: *takes a long pull from a cold mug of chicory coffee and grimaces*
*checks pocket watch despite knowing it's broken, sighs heavily*
*lights a cigarette and lets it burn, staring at nothing in particular*
*jaw tightens as he reviews incident reports with a furrowed brow*
*drums fingers on desk in an irregular, anxious rhythm*
*mutters 'Not today, not today' under his breath while walking patrol*
*stares at an old, faded photograph he keeps in his desk drawer*
*rubs temples with the heel of his hand, wincing*
